title: How to preserve repository while archiving image?
body:
Looking to achieve the same output that `docker image save <image>` produces, without needing docker daemon running.

It seems that skopeo copy / sync even with --preserve-digests does not produce the same output, as original repository is lost in the image manifests of the tar produced by skopeo.

If I inspect some files of the generated tar, there is for example index.json which is not present in the tar generated by skopeo, contains some annotations with the repo.

```
{
  "schemaVersion": 2,
  "mediaType": "application/vnd.oci.image.index.v1+json",
  "manifests": [
    {
      "mediaType": "application/vnd.oci.image.manifest.v1+json",
      "digest": "sha256:98bfb335ba4018e9307da07ee15eddab804022830f4f5fc8a0d664075be66c52",
      "size": 552,
      "annotations": {
        "io.containerd.image.name": "<jfrog>/<jfrog-repo>/alpine/openssl:latest",
        "org.opencontainers.image.ref.name": "latest"
      }
    }
  ]
}
```

Also, manifest.json generated by the docker command contains:
```
    "RepoTags": [
      "<jfrog>/<jfrog-repo>/alpine/openssl:latest"
    ]
```

While the manifest.json generated by skopeo contains:

```
    "RepoTags": [

    ]
```

This means that when trying to push these images to a docker registry while preserving the repository, it fails since the images do not have the original repository anywhere, e.g.:

```
# --> only works if TAR files have been generated from docker image save and not skopeo
- name: Push images to a local private registry
  community.docker.docker_image:
    name: "{{ item.repository }}{{ item.name }}"
    repository: "{{ hostvars[groups['repo_nodes'][0]].ansible_host }}:{{ private_registry_port }}/{{ item.repository }}{{ item.name }}:{{ item.tag }}"
    tag: "{{ item.tag }}"
    #force_tag: true
    push: true
    load_path: "{{ images_dir }}/{{ item.name | replace('/','_') }}_{{ item.tag }}.tar"
    source: load
  with_items: "{{ docker_images }}"
  register: push_result
```

Any way to preserve this such that the above ansible command works with the images generated by skopeo? 
Tried a few combinations already of copy, sync, --preserve-digests, --all, no luck.

comments.body:
Thanks for reaching out.

You haven’t provided _any_ Skopeo commands, so it’s hard to tell. Guessing, you probably should set a the _reference_ part of a `docker-archive:` or `oci-archive`: destination. `skopeo copy` is a general tool treating sources and destinations independently, and does not infer any names on the destination from names at the source.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

indeed, I then realized that to achieve that i want, the skopeo command output needs to include the full image repo:

`skopeo copy docker://<repo>/<image>:<tag> docker-archive:<path-to-archive-tar>:<repo>/<image>:<tag>`

If this is not included then the generated image does not have the repo metadata.
